\documentclass{article}
\usepackage{amsmath}

\title{A Paper with Correct Citations}
\author{Test Author}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
We survey attention mechanisms in neural machine translation.
\end{abstract}

\section{Introduction}
\label{sec:intro}

Neural machine translation has seen rapid progress in recent years.
The transformer model \cite{vaswani2017} introduced self-attention as the primary mechanism,
replacing recurrence entirely.

\section{Related Work}
\label{sec:related}

Bahdanau et al.\ introduced additive attention for sequence-to-sequence models \cite{bahdanau2015}.
The transformer architecture by Vaswani et al.\ \cite{vaswani2017} uses multi-head self-attention
to capture dependencies regardless of distance in the input sequence.

\section{Conclusion}
\label{sec:conclusion}

We surveyed the development of attention mechanisms in NMT.

\begin{thebibliography}{9}
\bibitem{vaswani2017} Vaswani, A., Shazeer, N., Parmar, N., Uszkoreit, J., Jones, L., Gomez, A.N., Kaiser, L. and Polosukhin, I. (2017). Attention Is All You Need. Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 30.
\bibitem{bahdanau2015} Bahdanau, D., Cho, K. and Bengio, Y. (2015). Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate. Proceedings of ICLR.
\end{thebibliography}

\end{document}
